title: Joint Operations
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Joint Operations
text:
Learning and remembering under fire: Proposals for a novel doctrinal system for Ukraine
This report examines doctrine and technology for Ukraine. It examines the role of doctrine for militaries and how other countries use doctrine, and ends with recommendations for action to create a doctrinal system suited for Ukraine's purposes.
What Is the U.S. Doing to Counter China in the Indo-Pacific?
RAND’s Kristen Gunness breaks down U.S. strategy in the Indo-Pacific. She discusses Washington's efforts to increase U.S. basing and access in the region, what actions might trigger a response from China, and the possibility that Beijing could impose a quarantine of Taiwan.
Metaverse: A New Domain for Joint All-Domain Operations
Key characteristics of metaverses may support Joint All-Domain Operations (JADO) and offer potential as a new domain for warfare. This paper outlines key characteristics of metaverses and how they map to defining aspects of JADO.
Ho'okele Mua II, a Wargame on Climate Change and Operational Risk in INDOPACOM
This report describes a wargame intended to aid U.S. Indo-Pacific Command operational planning by allowing players to explore the extent of operational risk that climate change could impose on the joint force in the Indo-Pacific region in the 2040s.
Better Than the Sum of Its Parts: Making NATO Reserves an Alliance Resource
NATO countries are trying to recruit and retain more service members, both active and reserve. And NATO is attempting to make its formations increasingly interoperable and multinational. Integrating reserve capabilities could help address both goals.
Organizing for Information Warfare at the Geographic Combatant Commands: Lessons from the United States Central Command Joint Effects Process
This report documents the planning processes used at U.S. Central Command (2020–2023) to better integrate information into its battle rhythm. It includes an assessment, lessons, and a framework for future learning.
Managing Escalation: Lessons and Challenges from Three Historical Crises Between Nuclear-Armed Powers
Using three historical case studies, the authors examine the ability of past decisionmakers to identify adversary thresholds and to apply this information to control escalation during militarized crises between nuclear-armed states.
Rethinking Jointness? The Strategic Value of Jointness in Major Power Competition and Conflict
This report examines whether the assumption that jointness is inherently valuable to the U.S. military is correct, and if so, in what ways. Understanding how and why jointness is most valuable can help the U.S. military compete more effectively.
The Russo-Ukrainian War Has Bolstered Ukraine's Nonstate Alliance Network
Since 2014, militant groups from Russia, Belarus, Chechnya, and elsewhere have established themselves as allies of Ukraine in its fight against Russia and its aligned forces. Though alignment with these groups presents clear benefits in the near term, Kyiv should be cautious since these groups could turn on Ukraine at any time should their interests no longer align.
The Aftermath of a Great Power War
RAND researchers drew on historical great power wars to develop four scenarios illustrating how hypothetical wars between the United States and either China or Russia could produce unanticipated consequences — even for the state that is victorious.
